mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2012 payroll growth of 80,000 persons signals labor market weakness
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with improving labor demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with payroll growth > 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or near zero combined with stable inflation below 2.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants price in sustained low-rate environment.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2012 FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and CPI at 228.527 index
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1% signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion, which supports corporate earnings and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 INDPRO at 97.2853 index, revised to 97.6609
regime_hint: any regime with positive supply-side momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher in months with INDPRO growth > 0.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2012 UNRATE at 8.2%, elevated from post-crisis lows
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or stalled recovery
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 700,000 units annualized combined with retail sales growth signals broad-based demand expansion that supports equity valuations through increased corporate earnings and multiplier effects over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 700,000 annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 HOUST at 708,000 units, RSAFS at 404,596 million
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rising above 2.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5% signals monetary policy accommodation lagging inflation, which erodes real returns and produces negative equity valuations over the next 20 trading days as market reprices inflation risk.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2012 FEDFUNDS at 0.16% with CPI at 228.527
regime_hint: monetary policy lag during inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly above 2.5% annualized combined with sustained payroll employment growth signals strong macroeconomic expansion that supports equity valuations through earnings growth expectations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 100,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2012 GDPC1 at 13,491.4 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: broad-based economic expansion with synchronized demand and labor growth
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window when both conditions are met.
